When I started my new business in January 2025, I had multiple people recommend QuickBooks to me. I was told it was user-friendly, easy to use, and one of the best systems for payroll and taxes. In the beginning, setup was difficult, but I worked through it because I trusted the platform. By March, I discovered that payroll taxes were no longer being paid properly. I began calling customer service repeatedly — sometimes 3 to 4 times per week — trying to get answers. Many of these calls lasted several hours, only for me to be transferred from one representative to another without any real resolution. Every person seemed to tell me something different, and nobody could explain what was actually happening with my account. I was repeatedly told that my account was “under review,” but no one could explain why or provide a timeline for fixing the issue. At one point, I believe my account may have been compromised. One of my employees’ pay settings had been changed from salary to an hourly wage at an absurd amount (around $300/hour), and there was also another unauthorized user listed on the account that I did not add or approve. I brought these concerns up multiple times, but I never received clear answers or meaningful help. Meanwhile, payroll taxes were not being paid properly, and I ended up receiving approximately $600 in penalties because of it. As a small business owner trying to do everything correctly, this was incredibly stressful and costly. What frustrates me most is the lack of support and accountability. I paid a significant amount for a service that caused more problems than it solved. Every attempt to get help resulted in more transfers, more waiting, and no real answers. I cannot recommend QuickBooks Payroll based on my experience. Small business owners rely on these services to handle critical financial responsibilities correctly, and unfortunately, my experience was extremely disappointing.

The software functions, as do many others. But I did not want customers paying electronically. So I turned off the CC and ACH payments. Then when they updated the app they automatically opted all users in for their customers paying electronically via ACH and a $25 “convenience” fee. Not realizing this change had been made, I sent an invoice to a customer. They paid a $150 invoice with a $25 fee. After hours on the phone with customer service, scouring the app, website, mobile and on the laptop, customer service and customer service from the office of the president informed me there is no way to refund my customer. They signed me up even though I had opted out and didn’t have a linked bank account. Now they recommend I link the account, rather than wait a month for it to be rejected after they earn a bit of interest on it (probably less than their $25 fee). Once I get my money I’m going to start testing out alternative software. What a total scam.
